resources.string: Hold keys in the vault and let a session — or another program on this machine — sign with them, without ever handing over the key itself.
When off, the agent answers every request with an empty key list.
No keys in the keychain yet. Add a private key or certificate in Vault.
Serve local programs (ssh, git, scp) over SSH_AUTH_SOCK.
The socket could not be opened. Check that the config directory is writable.
This system has no unix sockets with owner-only access, so the agent socket is unavailable.
The agent has not been used yet.
Hold keys in the vault and let a session sign with them, without ever handing over the key itself.
Turn the agent on above to serve local programs.
No keys in the agent — add one below
Ask before each use of a key, like ssh-agent -c. Unanswered requests are refused.
%1$s wants to sign with the key “%2$s”.
No answer within two minutes refuses the request.
This is what the agent holds. Which of these a forwarded host may use is chosen in the host itself.
